modal.title: Create new instance
modal.description: Your new instance starts empty. Configure WooCommerce, Meilisearch, and other integrations from Configuration after you create it.

errors.invalidName: Enter a name with at least one letter or number.
errors.saveFailed: Could not create instance. Try again.

inspector.summarySelectStage: Select a stage to see details.

maintenance.title: Flow maintenance
maintenance.description: Edit the shared flow definitions. Changes affect every instance using this flow.

flowWarnings.title: Flow validation
flowWarnings.healthy: Flow is complete. All required stages are present and every transition references valid endpoints.

transitions.title: Transitions
transitions.deleteWarning: Deleting the transition will also remove its values from every dataset (cascade).

datasetValues.title: Dataset transition values
datasetValues.noDataset: This instance has no active dataset.

benchmarks.title: Benchmark sources
benchmarks.noValues: The dataset has no values yet. Create values before adding sources.

frictionPoints.title: Friction points
frictionPoints.platformManagedNote: Friction-point definitions are managed at the platform level. Read-only here.

frictionFindings.title: Friction findings
frictionFindings.templateReadOnly: This instance is a template. Findings are read-only.

actions.confirmDelete: Delete this record? This action cannot be undone.

algolia.title: Algolia Settings
algolia.description: Credentials and index settings for this instance only.

agentPanel.title: Assistant
agentPanel.noActivity: No agent activity in this session.
agentPanel.hint: Agent narrations will appear here while it works.
agentPanel.copyHint: Copy the full response to clipboard

activityStream.seePanel: See details in the Assistant panel.

inherited.label: Inherited from parent categories
inherited.desc: These attributes are defined in parent categories. You can override their settings here.

own.label: Defined in {categoryName}
own.empty: This category has no own attributes.

note.label: Context note for the agent
note.placeholder: E.g.: "Words like Medium, Large refer to breed size, not product size."

empty.selectPrompt: Select an attribute or create a new one.

fields.name: Name
fields.codeAutoHint: Generated from the name. You can edit it before saving — it locks afterward.
fields.codeManualHint: Manually edited. It will lock when saved.
fields.codeLockedHint: The code cannot be changed after creating the attribute.
fields.descriptionPlaceholder: What this attribute is used for…
fields.parsingHintPlaceholder: e.g. "Set true when the name contains 'Prescription Diet' or codes R/D, K/D, C/D, J/D, U/D, Z/D, W/D, I/D, L/D, Derm Complete"
fields.parsingHintDesc: Free-text the import agent reads. Use it to teach the agent how to recognize this attribute in product names. Takes priority over naive matching from the attribute name alone.

behavior.isMultivalueDesc: A product can have multiple values
behavior.isActiveDesc: Only active attributes are used in imports

actions.confirmDelete: Delete this attribute? This action cannot be undone.

variantAxis.inheritedDesc: These axes were defined in parent categories and are automatically available.
variantAxis.ownDesc: These axes are added to the inherited ones. The import agent will use the full pool to detect variants in product names.
variantAxis.notePlaceholder: E.g.: "Words like Medium, Large refer to breed size, not product size."
variantAxis.previewLabel: Agent preview — full pool for this category

noResults.title: Searches without results
noResults.description: Terms your customers searched for but didn't find. Adding a synonym connects the term to existing products.
noResults.empty: No searches without results in this period.
noResults.notConfigured: Algolia is not configured for this instance.

catalogAlerts.title: Catalog alerts
catalogAlerts.placeholder: Coming soon: catalog alerts from the import engine.

synonym.note: Bidirectional synonym: both terms will find the same products.

empty.title: No products match this filter.
empty.sub: Try adjusting the filters or creating a new product.

create.title: New product
create.subhead: Brand, category, variants and pricing — all in one screen.
create.variantsHelp: Generate variants from axes (weight, size) or enter them manually
create.manualEmpty: No variants yet. Add one manually.

attributes.subhead: Fill in the attributes specific to the selected categories.

photos.subhead: Add photo URLs. Drag or use the arrows to reorder. The first photo is the primary by default.
photos.emptySub: Add image URLs for this product
photos.techDebtBody: Direct file upload is planned for a future phase. For now, use hosted image URLs.

axes.none: No attributes defined. Create attributes first.

sidebar.guide1: Start with the name and pick a product type. Brand and categories help classify it.
sidebar.guide2: Variants are generated by combining the values of the axes you choose. You can also add them manually.
sidebar.guide3: After saving, you'll be able to edit images, detailed attributes and inventory.

variants.photosSub: These photos override the product primary when this variant is shown on its own (storefront, WooCommerce sync, search index).

wizard.title: Import products from a file
wizard.subtitle: Upload an Excel or CSV. The agent classifies, groups and proposes variants; you review and confirm.

agentPanel.title: Agent conversation
agentPanel.emptyState: You'll see what the agent is doing and what it returns at each step here.
agentPanel.copyHint: Copy the full response to clipboard

step1.label: File
step1.title: Source file
step1.subtitle: Accepts .xlsx, .xls and .csv (max 10 MB). The agent processes the first sheet.
step1.dropZone: Drop a file here or click to choose
step1.agentBodyParsed: Read «{file}»: {cols} columns, {rows} rows. When you're ready, pick categories in the next step.

step2.label: Brand & category
step2.brandHint: Applies to every product in the file. If the brand doesn't exist yet, create it in Catalog → Brands first.
step2.llmExtractionHint: If on, the agent extracts axes (weight, flavor) in the next step.
step2.analysisHint: Pick the candidate categories these products could belong to. They can be from different branches of the tree; each branch implicitly includes its subcategories. The agent picks the best one for each product within that set.
step2.multiCandidateHint: The agent will choose from {n} categories (your picks plus their subcategories).
step2.singleCandidateHint: Only one category resolved; the agent will be skipped and every product will be assigned to it.
step2.singleCandidateReasoning: Only category in the candidate set; direct assignment.
step2.candidatesRequired: Pick at least one candidate category before analyzing.
step2.noNamesFound: No valid names in the selected column.
step2.noSuggestion: The agent did not propose a category for this product.
step2.nameColumnRequired: Pick the name column before analyzing.
step2.agentBodySkipped: Only one candidate category («{category}»). Assigned all {n} products directly without calling the agent.
step2.agentBodyAnalyzing: Asked the agent to classify {n} products against {c} candidate categories.
step2.agentBodyAnalyzed: Done: {n} products analyzed — {high} high confidence, {medium} medium, {low} low, {unmatched} no suggestion. Model: {model}.

step3.label: Group variants
step3.title: Group products into bases + variants
step3.subtitle: The agent reads each category's vocabulary (axes and attributes) and groups rows into base products with their variants.
step3.successAlert: {bases} base products · {variants} variants. Axis columns are highlighted; every cell is editable.
step3.noCategoriesPicked: No categories were assigned in the previous step.
step3.agentBodyGrouping: Asked the agent to group products into bases with their variants for {categories} categories.
step3.sourceNameTooltip: Original name as it came in the source file. Use it to verify the agent's interpretation.
step3.statUnaccounted: {n, plural, one {# unaccounted row} other {# unaccounted rows}}
step3.unaccountedTitle: {n, plural, one {# unaccounted row} other {# unaccounted rows}} — review so the math adds up.
step3.emptyVocabHint: The category "{category}" has no attributes defined. Define weight, flavor, size, etc. in Catalog → Categories to capture them here on future imports.

step4.label: Mapping
step4.title: Map columns to GroLabs fields
step4.subtitle: Drag file columns (right) onto GroLabs fields (left). Each side shows what the other is bound to, so you can read the pairing from either direction.
step4.autoHint: Obvious matches (sku, barcode, price…) are pre-suggested. Change anything that's wrong.
step4.lockedFromStep2: Set in Step 2; change it there if needed.

err.title: There {n, plural, one {is # error} other {are # errors}} to fix before continuing:

step6.label: Import
step6.title: Ready to import
step6.ready: Will create {bases} products with {variants} variants total.
step6.noProductType: No product type configured for this instance. Create one in Catalog → Product types before importing.
step6.nothingToImport: No products are assigned to a category.

hintReview.title: The agent learned new things
hintReview.subtitle: Accept the terms so the agent recognizes them next time. Optional — you can ignore this and move on.

woocommerce.pageDescriptionConfigured: Pull categories and products from {siteUrl} into GroLabs. Imports are re-runnable: already-imported records are updated, not duplicated.
woocommerce.pageDescriptionMissing: Configure WooCommerce in Configuration → WooCommerce before importing.

notConfigured.title: WooCommerce is not configured
notConfigured.body: Save credentials in Configuration → WooCommerce and return here.

algolia.pageDescription: Connect your Algolia account to enable real-time search on your catalog.

woocommerce.pageDescription: Connect your WooCommerce store to push products to the storefront.

fields.siteUrlHint: E.g. https://yourstore.com (no /wp-admin). Site must serve HTTPS with the WooCommerce plugin active.
fields.consumerSecretHint: Generate a key/secret pair in WooCommerce → Settings → Advanced → REST API with read/write permissions.

toast.missingFields: URL and consumer key are required
toast.missingSecret: Enter the consumer secret or click Cancel to keep the saved one
toast.testNeedsSecret: To test, enter the secret or click Replace

search.pageDescription: Meilisearch connection, authorized storefront domains, and index status.

instanceId.label: Instance ID
instanceId.help: Paste this ID into the WordPress plugin to connect your storefront.

domains.label: Authorized domains
domains.help: Search requests will only be accepted from these hosts. e.g. shop.wazu.gt

index.label: Search index
index.help: Create or update this instance’s Meilisearch index with default settings (searchable attributes, synonyms, stop words).

indexing.label: Indexing status
indexing.help: Compares GroLabs's catalog against documents in Meilisearch. Reindex rebuilds the entire index for this instance.
indexing.confirmReindex: This rebuilds the entire search index for this instance. May take several minutes. Continue?

toast.duplicateDomain: That domain is already on the list

ga4.pageDescription: Connect your Google Analytics 4 property to power the traffic dashboard and daily alerts.

preConnect.intro: Connect a GA4 property (read-only) to pull daily traffic metrics and enable health alerts.
preConnect.bulletDaily: Sessions, users, engagement, top pages — refreshed every 6 hours.
preConnect.bulletAlerts: Auto-alerts when sessions, engagement, or channel mix change meaningfully.
preConnect.bulletRealtime: Live active-users widget on the traffic dashboard.

fields.propertyIdHint: Numeric ID of your GA4 property (Admin → Property settings). Digits only.

disconnect.confirm: Disconnect Google Analytics? This deletes the stored token and stops pulls.

oauthErrors.no_refresh_token: Google did not return a refresh token.

sync.title: Product sync
sync.subtitle: Push products to Algolia (search) and WooCommerce (storefront).

notConfigured.algolia: Algolia is not configured for this instance.
notConfigured.woocommerce: WooCommerce is not configured for this instance.

bulk.selected: {n, plural, one {# product selected} other {# products selected}}

empty.title: No products visible
empty.sub: Adjust the filters or create products first.

mappingModal.title: Field mapping
mappingModal.subtitle: How GroLabs projects each product/variant onto Algolia and WooCommerce. For v1 the mapping is read-only.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Manage cost lists, pricing policies and WooCommerce sync

empty.title: No active batches
empty.text: Import a price list to create a change batch

stub.comingSoonText: This view is part of the pricing module and will ship in a follow-up.

calculationMode.title: Calculation method
calculationMode.subtitle: How GroLabs calculates selling prices from cost. This setting drives every percentage in the pricing module.

margin.label: Margin
margin.description: Percentage is profit as a share of the selling price.

markup.label: Markup
markup.description: Percentage is profit as a share of cost.

defaults.minHint: Below this level a row is flagged critical.

maxPriceChange.title: Maximum price change
maxPriceChange.subtitle: Flags batch rows whose new price differs from the current selling price by more than the threshold. Applies in both directions — increases and decreases.
maxPriceChange.thresholdHint: Changes greater than or equal to this value trigger the warning.
maxPriceChange.toastInvalidPct: Threshold must be a valid number ≥ 0

subtitle.margin: Each category can inherit its parent's percentages, or set its own targets.
subtitle.markup: Each category can inherit its parent's markup, or set its own.

categoryMargins.inheritedCount: {count, plural, one {# category inherits} other {# categories inherit}} from parent
categoryMargins.usingDefaultsCount: {count, plural, one {# uses default} other {# use defaults}}
categoryMargins.empty: No categories. Create categories in the catalogue to assign margins.

toast.invalidTarget: Target must be a valid number ≥ 0
toast.invalidMin: Minimum must be a valid number ≥ 0

charmRules.title: Charm-pricing rules
charmRules.subtitle: Automatically snap a calculated price to a more attractive value within a band.
charmRules.empty: No rules yet. Add the first one to start applying charm prices in change batches.

testCalculator.label: Test price:
testCalculator.noMatch: No active band covers this price.
testCalculator.enterPrice: Enter a price to see the result.

ends_in.label: Ends in (cents)
ends_in.hint: Snaps to the next value whose decimal part matches. E.g. 0.99, 0.95.

ends_in_whole.label: Ends in (whole)
ends_in_whole.hint: Snaps the integer portion up to the next number ending in this value. E.g. 9, 99, 95.

round_to.label: Round to multiple
round_to.hint: Rounds up to the nearest multiple.

fixed_offset.label: Fixed offset
fixed_offset.hint: Rounds up to the next whole, then subtracts this value.

charmRules.confirmDelete: Delete this rule? Already-synced batches are not affected.

toast.invalidMin: From must be a valid number ≥ 0
toast.invalidMax: To must be ≥ From, or empty for no limit
toast.invalidValue: Strategy value must be a valid number ≥ 0
toast.invalidWholeValue: "Ends in (whole)" value must be an integer ≥ 1 (e.g. 9, 99, 95)
toast.conflictTitle: Rule saved but overlaps with another
toast.conflictWinnerOther: The existing rule wins because it has a lower sort order
toast.conflictWinnerThis: This rule wins because it has a lower sort order

pendingLists.title: Pending price lists
pendingLists.subtitle: Imported lists that haven't yet been turned into a batch.
pendingLists.empty: No pending lists. Import one to get started.

batches.title: Recent batches
batches.subtitle: Each batch is a proposed set of price changes calculated from a price list.

empty.title: No batches yet
empty.text: Create your first batch from a pending list. If you don't have any lists, import one first with the button above.

batchDetail.staleBanner: Configuration changed since this batch was created. Recompute to apply the new values.

buttons.markReadyBlockedHint: Resolve the critical rows before marking as ready.

toast.invalidCharm: Charm price must be a valid number ≥ 0
toast.invalidFinal: Final price must be a valid number ≥ 0
toast.bulkDeleteConfirm: Delete {n} rows from this batch? The original price list is untouched.

batchDetail.emptyItems: This batch has no items. That usually means none of the list rows matched a catalogue variant.
batchDetail.emptyAfterFilters: No items match the active filters.

notConfigured.title: WooCommerce isn't configured
notConfigured.text: Configure WooCommerce credentials so GroLabs can push approved prices.

ready.title: Batches ready to sync
ready.subtitle: Each batch pushes its final prices to WooCommerce. If a product fails, the batch returns to 'Ready' so you can retry.
ready.empty: No batches in 'Ready' status. Mark a batch as ready from its worksheet.

history.title: Sync history
history.subtitle: Every attempt, successful or failed, is logged here.
history.empty: No batches have been synced yet.

toast.partial: Partial: {ok} ok, {fail} failed. Batch returned to 'Ready'.

violationsPage.queueSubtitle: Violations detected in change batches show up here for approval or rejection.
violationsPage.queueEmpty: No violations yet. They'll appear once a batch has rows in critical state.

authQueue.title: Authorization queue
authQueue.subtitle: Warning and critical rows across every batch that hasn't synced yet.
authQueue.empty: No open violations. When a batch produces warning or critical rows, they'll appear here.
authQueue.emptyAfterFilters: No rows match the active filters.

mapRules.title: Price rules (MAP / max)
mapRules.subtitle: Rules that set the minimum or maximum allowed price per brand or provider. They can apply to every variant from a source or a specific one.
mapRules.empty: No rules yet. Add the first to start enforcing minimum and maximum prices.
mapRules.confirmDelete: Delete this rule? Already-synced batches are not affected.

variantPicker.placeholder: Search by name, SKU or barcode…

fields.minPriceHint: Selling price cannot be below this.

ruleTypeHint.MAP_min: Sets a minimum selling price only.
ruleTypeHint.max_price: Sets a maximum selling price only.

toast.invalidMin: Min price must be a valid number ≥ 0
toast.invalidMax: Max price must be a valid number ≥ 0

empty.title: No providers yet
empty.text: Add your first provider to start importing cost lists. You can edit all their details later.

brands.hint: Search and select the brands this provider distributes. You can choose multiple.
brands.empty: No brands registered yet. Create brands in the catalogue before linking them.

providerForm.toastBrandsError: Provider saved, but brand assignment failed

importDialog.title: Import price list

description.upload: Upload the provider's Excel or CSV file.
description.configure: Pick the provider and map the columns. We've suggested the ones we detected from the headers.
description.result: The list was imported. Here are the results.

importDialog.dropZone: Drag the file here, or click to choose

fields.suggestedPriceColumnHint: Optional. Shown as a reference in the batch.
